license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfh4jj8w
title: Protein expression on the surface of Escherichia coli
author: Maja Rennig

steps_raw.instruction: Pick a fresh colony of your BL21 (DE3) strain with your expression vector, and inoculate it in LB supplemented with relevant antibiotics. Grow the culture at 37 °C at 250 RPM shaking Overnight . The volume of the overnight culture depends on the volume of the expression culture and should be at least 1/100 of the expression culture
Dilute the overnight culture 1:100 in fresh LB supplemented with relevant antibiotics
Grow the culture at 37 °C with 250 RPM shaking until an OD 600 = 0.5 - 0.6
Induce expression by adding L-rhamnose to a final concentration of 5 millimolar (mM)
Let the culture grow at 30 °C with 250 RPM shaking for up to 20:00:00
Spin the culture down at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes and discard the supernatant
Resuspend pellet in the desired assay buffer (e.g. 10 millimolar (mM) - 50 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl)
Run activity assay in liquid or on agar plates.
Spin the culture down at 4000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes and discard the supernatant
Resuspend pellet in 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl or 100 µL /ODU TEV cleavage buffer ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl pH 7.5 , 150 millimolar (mM) NaCl 2 , 0.5 millimolar (mM) EDTA)
Add purified and activated TEV protease
Incubate at Room temperature Overnight (~ 16:00:00 ) It is also possible to shorten the incubation time, so that the protein harvest can still be performed on the same day.
Spin the cells at 5000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and collect the supernatant
Keep the extractions On ice when working with it and at 4 °C for storage
